step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with agent1’s assessment but would add another point to support why option B is indeed the most appropriate answer among those given:

Both zinc oxide eugenol cement and polycarboxylate cement share the characteristic of being able to adhere to tooth structures through some form of interaction—whether via a mechanical lock-in mechanism as seen in ZOE cements or more direct chemical bonding as observed in polycarboxylates. This adherence property makes them suitable for various dental procedures where retention against dislodgement is critical. Therefore, my final agreement stands with Option B.
